steps.title: Identify the clause structures
steps.content: ”They are hardly pristine” is an independent clause. The word “though” is a subordinating conjunction, making any clause it introduces dependent on the first clause.

steps.title: Apply the rule for punctuating subordinating conjunctions
steps.content: The correct punctuation places a comma before the subordinating conjunction “though,” producing “pristine, though” with the dependent clause continuing uninterrupted. The other options violate this pattern by using a semicolon (which doesn't precede subordinating conjunctions), combining punctuation redundantly, or adding an unnecessary comma after “though.” This matches choice D—D is correct.
